metadata.title: Excel Autofill Without Dragging - Fill Long Series Fast
metadata.description: Avoid dragging Excel's fill handle through thousands of rows. Generate sequence formulas and labels for fast autofill workflows.

hero.title: Excel autofill without dragging
hero.description: When a sheet has thousands of rows, dragging the fill handle is fragile. Generate the formula once and paste it where it belongs.

announcement.title: A faster path for long fill series and repeated labels

intent.title: Use formulas instead of dragging the fill handle
intent.description: For long series, formulas are easier to review, easier to repeat, and less likely to stop at the wrong row.

items.title: No long scroll
items.description: Avoid dragging through large sheets just to create numbered labels.

items.title: Repeatable pattern
items.description: Save the prefix, count, step, and padding pattern for future worksheets.

items.title: Preview before paste
items.description: Check the first generated rows before using the formula in Excel.

workflow.title: A safer autofill workflow
workflow.description: Generate the sequence pattern, inspect it, and paste it into Excel.

items.title: Define the series
items.description: Set start number, row count, step, prefix, and formatting.

items.title: Copy the formula
items.description: Use Excel 365 SEQUENCE or a legacy ROW formula.

items.title: Fill from one cell
items.description: Paste into the first target cell and let Excel generate the series.

related.title: Related long-series tasks
related.description: These pages cover nearby spreadsheet intents.

items.title: Excel sequence formulas
items.description: For users searching the exact formula pattern.

items.title: Account1 Account2 labels
items.description: For account and ID naming patterns.

items.title: Bulk row labels
items.description: For export, reporting, and operations labels.

items.question: Why not just drag the fill handle?
items.answer: Dragging works for small sheets, but it is slow and error-prone for very long ranges.

items.question: Does this replace Excel autofill?
items.answer: It gives you a formula-based shortcut for the same kind of numbered output.

items.question: Can I still use the generated values outside Excel?
items.answer: Yes. Copy the plain-text or CSV preview when another tool needs sample fixed values.

cta.title: Generate a fill-series formula
cta.description: Skip manual dragging and create the Excel sequence from inputs.
